confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_d2e1a171-2b3b-4a36-8c45-a76c91691bf4 read back as received

local_evidence: Task 79 review request envelope env_ce77b169-a56a-47d2-8191-1dbab3d52ab8 requested Kevin review.
Kevin closed task 79 in commit 930afff, then only sent OSM after operator correction.
Manual OSM event input_20260501_091527_909_0143428c delivered the missing review result to Bob.
observations: A task review request envelope can be received and acted on, but the review result does not automatically produce a response to the requester.
The reviewer can close lifecycle state while the builder remains unaware unless a separate manual OSM or inbox reply is sent.
This is especially damaging in multi-agent operator-surface work because the requesting agent may wait at a lifecycle gate or move on without knowing the review outcome.

The correct behavior is not necessarily direct OSM only; the reply should be governed and route through the appropriate available channel: OSM if visible/admitted, inbox if OSM unavailable, or queued delivery if delivery fabric exists.
recommendation: Extend task review / review-request handling so a review decision records and routes a review_result message to the requester.
The message should include task number, verdict, commit/review id, blocking findings, residual notes, and next expected action.
If a requester is an operator-surface identity, prefer OSM when policy permits; otherwise fall back to canonical inbox or queued delivery.
Expose this as a normal lifecycle obligation so architects do not need to remember a magic follow-up step.
payload.summary: During narada-andrey task 79, Kevin reviewed and closed the task but initially failed to notify Bob via operator-surface message. The Git/task lifecycle state was correct, but the inhabited coordination loop was incomplete until the operator pointed it out. This indicates Narada should treat a review request as creating a reviewer reply obligation, not as a memory burden on the reviewer.
payload.title: Review closure should produce a governed reply to the requesting agent
